name: Beaches Route
name_original: Santa Eulària des Riu desde Sant Carles de Peralta
description: Enjoy an easy 10.7-mile touring cycling route from Sant Carles de Peralta to Santa Eulària des Riu, exploring scenic valleys and beaches.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Santa Eulalia Del Río navigate a landscape characterized by its Mediterranean coastline, gentle hills, and pine forests. The region offers a mix of coastal paths and inland trails, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Many routes feature views of the sea and pass through agricultural areas. The elevation changes are generally moderate, making it accessible for different cycling abilities. Best touring cycling routes around Santa Eulalia Del Río The most popular touring cycling route is Beaches Route, a 10.7 miles (17.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 3 minutes to complete. This easy route explores the coastal areas and various beaches of Santa Eulalia Del Río. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Es Canar Beach – Cala Nova Beach loop from Santa Eulària des Riu, a moderate 20.8 miles (33.6 km) path. This circular bike route offers scenic views along the coastline between two popular beaches. Local touring cyclists also love the Restaurante Bar Anita – View of Cala de Sant Vicent loop from Santa Eulària des Riu, a 29.0 miles (46.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 2 hours 56 minutes. Touring cycling around Santa Eulalia Del Río is defined by coastal paths, pine-covered hills, and charming villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy touring bike trails to more advanced bike trails. The routes in Santa Eulalia Del Río are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 140 reviews. More than 1700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Santa Eulalia Del Río's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Santa Eulalia Del Río?
A: There are over 150 touring cycling routes around Santa Eulalia Del Río, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Santa Eulalia Del Río?
A: Yes, Santa Eulalia Del Río offers 24 easy touring cycling routes. A great option for beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013131667/beaches-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beaches Route&lt;/a&gt;, which is 10.7 miles (17.3 km) long and explores the coastal areas and various beaches with moderate elevation changes.

Q: Can I find family-friendly touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Many of the easy routes are suitable for families, featuring gentle terrain and scenic coastal paths. These routes often pass by beaches and offer opportunities for stops. Always check the route details for specific elevation and surface information to ensure it meets your family's needs.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes around Santa Eulalia Del Río?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38195618" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Es Canar Beach – Cala Nova Beach loop from Santa Eulària des Riu&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 20.8-mile (33.6 km) circular path offering scenic views between two popular beaches.

Q: What options are there for more experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge?
A: For experienced cyclists, there are 60 difficult touring cycling routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/707278" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Restaurante Bar Anita – View of Cala de Sant Vicent loop from Santa Eulària des Riu&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 29.0-mile (46.7 km) trail with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 3 hours.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Santa Eulalia Del Río?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Santa Eulalia Del Río is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these months, the weather is pleasantly warm, and the tourist crowds are smaller than in peak summer. Summers can be very hot, making cycling less comfortable, while winters are mild but can be wetter.

Q: What interesting sights or landmarks can I explore along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer views of the Mediterranean Sea and pass through charming villages. You can also explore historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/380673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibiza Old Town (Dalt Vila)&lt;/a&gt; or natural attractions such as various caves, including &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5847493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova des Forn&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1757634" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Blanca Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;, which are often near cycling paths.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming or visiting beaches along the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Many touring cycling routes in Santa Eulalia Del Río hug the coastline, providing easy access to numerous beaches and coves perfect for a refreshing swim. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013131667/beaches-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beaches Route&lt;/a&gt; are specifically designed to explore these coastal gems.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Santa Eulalia Del Río?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscape, combining beautiful coastal views with tranquil inland trails through pine forests and agricultural areas, as well as the accessibility for various cycling abilities.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that pass by cafes or places to stop for refreshments?
A: Yes, many routes are designed to pass through or near villages and towns where you can find cafes, restaurants, and bars. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21944988" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Coastal Cliffs – Restaurante Bar Anita loop from Santa Eulària des Riu&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, includes a stop near Restaurante Bar Anita, a well-known local spot.

Q: Can I reach the touring cycling routes using public transport?
A: Santa Eulalia Del Río has a local bus network that connects to various points around the island. While some routes might be accessible directly by bus, it's advisable to check specific bus lines and their bike carriage policies, as these can vary. Many routes start directly from Santa Eulalia Del Río town center, which is well-served by public transport.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a touring cycling route?
A: Santa Eulalia Del Río offers several public parking areas, particularly near the town center and beaches. When planning your route, look for designated parking zones close to your chosen starting point. Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14684020" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibiza Old Town (Dalt Vila) – Cala Llonga Beach loop from Santa Eulària des Riu&lt;/a&gt;, originate from easily accessible locations with parking options nearby.
tips:
Beach located next to the town of Santa Eulalia, on the island of Ibiza. Beach 350 meters long and 50 wide with fine, clear sand. A good point to highlight is the number of services offered around it.

A short distance from the beach of Es Canar is the most important hippy market on the island, where you can find trinkets and some handmade products.

Municipality and town of the autonomous community of the Balearic Islands (Spain). Located in the eastern part of the island of Ibiza. In Muslim times the area was called Xarc.

The town arose around the church at the top of Puig de Missa. The current church is from the 16th century as well as the annexed tower from which the entire region could be defended with artillery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and terrain like for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is considered an easy touring bicycle ride, suitable for all fitness and skill levels. The terrain is characterized by mostly paved surfaces with minimal elevation gain, making it a comfortable experience for most cyclists.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and natural features will I encounter on the ride?
faqs.answer: You will experience picturesque valleys and wooded hills, characteristic of Santa Eulària des Riu. The route offers glimpses of Ibiza's natural charm, from the interior's beauty to beautiful bays. You can expect diverse landscapes throughout your journey.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is highly suitable for families and beginners. It is part of a network of routes in Santa Eulària des Riu recommended for families due to its low difficulty and easy-to-manage duration.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle from Sant Carles de Peralta to Santa Eulària des Riu?
faqs.answer: Thanks to Ibiza's pleasant climate, offering around 300 days of sunshine annually, this route is ideal for year-round cycle tourism. Spring and autumn offer particularly comfortable temperatures for cycling.

faqs.question: Are there any specific landmarks or attractions along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several points of interest. You'll pass by the beautiful Es Canar Beach and the central Plaça d'Espanya and Town Hall in Santa Eulària des Riu. Other notable sites in the area include Pou des Lleó, the Torre d'en Valls, Cala Boix, Cala Mastella, and the Peralta fountain.

faqs.question: Does this route connect to other cycling trails in the Santa Eulària des Riu area?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is part of 12 interconnected cycling routes in Santa Eulària des Riu. It also passes through sections of official trails such as Ruta Platges, El Camí IT4TR5 El Camí Illenc: de l'Alguer a Formentera, and Passatge Sant Jaume.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to access the start of this route in Sant Carles de Peralta?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the trailhead are not provided, Sant Carles de Peralta is a village with general parking available. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for the most convenient parking options.

faqs.question: Is the Santa Eulària des Riu from Sant Carles de Peralta route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The information available does not specifically address whether dogs are permitted on this particular cycling route. It's best to check with local tourism offices or signage for regulations regarding pets on cycling paths in the Santa Eulària des Riu municipality.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It is a publicly accessible cycling path within the municipality of Santa Eulària des Riu.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or get refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route often passes through villages and resorts, providing opportunities to stop for refreshments. Both Sant Carles de Peralta and Santa Eulària des Riu offer various cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel.

faqs.question: Can I rent a touring bicycle near Sant Carles de Peralta or Santa Eulària des Riu?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Sant Carles de Peralta and Santa Eulària des Riu are popular tourist areas, and you can typically find bike hire services in or near these towns, offering touring bicycles suitable for this route.
